newsSinceJun6: No material new news since June 6. The recent catalyst cluster all predates the window: Q2 FY26 beat-and-raise reported May 27 (stock fell ~8.6% despite raised full-year guidance), Elliott cooperation agreement adding Jesse Cohn to the board (announced May 27, effective June 1), and the expanded Samsung Foundry AI-EDA/IP collaboration across 2nm/3DIC announced at SAFE 2026 in late May. Today's -2.73% is continuation of post-earnings de-rating drift, not a new fundamental event; no fresh downgrades or contract news found for June 8-9.

sources.label: Sherwood News - Synopsys drops despite better-than-expected Q2 and guidance boost
Bloomberg - Synopsys settles with Elliott, names Jesse Cohn to board
PR Newswire - Synopsys appoints Jesse Cohn to Board of Directors
DCD - Synopsys extends Samsung Foundry partnership for AI and multi-die designs

verdicts.rankRationale: Yesterday's hold stands: nothing genuinely new since June 6, and on a crowding-sensitive alpha list an $87B, fully discovered EDA duopolist does not gain hidden-bottleneck status. The continued post-earnings sell-off against a beat-and-raise modestly improves entry asymmetry, worth a small positive drift but not a rank move.
verdicts.bull: Beat-and-raise Q2 with 42% revenue growth, Elliott on the board pushing value realization, and Samsung 2nm/3DIC expansion deepen the verification/chiplet moat just as the stock de-rates ~9% off the print.
verdicts.neutral: Quality bottleneck exposure is real but fully priced into consensus Buy ratings and a $560 average target; alpha here is mean-reversion beta, not discovery.
verdicts.bear: Post-earnings distribution despite good numbers signals positioning fatigue; Ansys integration debt load and China/regulatory exposure cap multiple recovery.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Elliott-driven margin/portfolio actions or a photonics/chiplet design-win disclosure that re-rates the AI-attach narrative.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued multiple compression in design software as investors rotate to harder physical bottlenecks (HBM, packaging, power).

verdicts.rankRationale: This page already moved SNPS up yesterday on the EDA/IP exposure evidence; no new information since June 6 justifies moving again. Today's -2.7% drift deepens the negative-YTD reset against raised guidance, which keeps the setup mildly improving but only warrants a small drift delta at the current rank.
verdicts.bull: Raised FY guidance, Samsung Foundry 2nm/3DIC certification breadth, and activist board oversight give fundamental support while the stock sits -4% YTD, an unusual reset for a structural AI design-complexity winner.
verdicts.neutral: Design-validation exposure is durable but the rerating-versus-fundamentals gap is modest; rank 50 already reflects the balanced bottleneck-vs-discovery tradeoff.
verdicts.bear: If hyperscaler custom-silicon programs consolidate or China revenue restrictions tighten, the raised guidance becomes the high-water mark and the de-rating proves fundamental rather than flow-driven.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Evidence of AI-driven EDA seat/IP pull-through in Q3 commentary or new foundry-node certification wins.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Ansys integration execution and leverage limiting buyback/margin flexibility during a software de-rating cycle.
